nonBlockingIssues.description: canEquipWeapon/canEquipArmor use `(item as any)` to access weaponType/type properties not on ItemInstance interface — type-unsafe but works at runtime
Mercenary movement is frame-rate dependent (0.06 tiles/frame) rather than delta-time based. Matches existing player movement pattern.
Only tank mercenaries can be targeted by monsters — other roles are effectively invulnerable in combat
Camp proximity check logic is duplicated between renderHirePanel and renderMercenaryInfo in UIScene
pet_void_butterfly (rare spawn pet) has no actual acquisition mechanism — defined as '稀有刷新' but no spawn points implemented. Pet is unreachable in normal gameplay.
English stat names displayed in pet UI (bonusStat shown raw: 'attackSpeed', 'defense', etc.) instead of Chinese labels. Violates Chinese UI text convention.
Feed error message shows raw item IDs (e.g., 'c_hp_potion_s') instead of Chinese item names
Boss pet drop table hardcoded inline in ZoneScene instead of being data-driven
Training ground building semantics silently changed from expBonus to mercExpBonus — existing saves get different behavior without notification

appliedUpdates.description: Added MercenarySystem and companion panel documentation to architecture.md (persistent system, P key panel, companion lifecycle)

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the UIScene ↔ ZoneScene cross-scene method pattern. Workers add public methods to ZoneScene that UIScene calls via `(this.zone as any)`. List known cross-scene methods to help workers understand the boundary.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mercenary-system added spawnMercenarySprite() and destroyMercenarySprite() as public methods on ZoneScene, called from UIScene via `as any` cast. This matches existing patterns but is undocumented.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: Do not change the semantics of existing building/stat bonuses without explicit migration or player notification. New behaviors should use new stat keys rather than repurposing existing ones.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: pet-system-overhaul changed training_ground from 'expBonus' (offline exp) to 'mercExpBonus' (mercenary exp). Players who upgraded training_ground for exp now silently get a different bonus.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note: The P key companion panel is the unified panel for all companion types (mercenaries, pets, and future companion features). New companion features should integrate into this panel rather than creating separate UI.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: pet-system-overhaul refactored the mercenary-only panel to a combined companions panel showing both mercenary and pets.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that ItemInstance lacks weaponType/itemType discriminator fields. Workers should use `as any` or base lookup via baseId to access these. Consider adding optional fields to ItemInstance in a future cleanup pass.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mercenary-system uses `(item as any).weaponType` and `(item as any).type` in equipment validation. This is a known type-safety gap affecting multiple systems.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker's skill feedback says procedure was followed correctly — no skill changes needed
rejectedObservations.reason: No action needed; positive feedback, not a change request

rejectedObservations.observation: Skill suggestion to check for pre-existing partial implementations from prior worker sessions
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a valid suggestion but is more of a general orchestration concern than a skill change. Workers already read handoffs which should cover this. The issue was specific to a multi-session feature, not a systemic pattern.
